target.title: v0.37.0: Overview menu stutters on every scroll event with multiple providers on macOS 27
target.body:
### What happens

The Overview tab feels janky while scrolling. It is not just a slow first open: every wheel scroll stutters when several provider cards are visible. I can reproduce it on my local latest install, and I have also seen the same behavior on a few remote machines running recent versions.

### Environment

- CodexBar: 0.37.0 (90)
- App path: `/Applications/CodexBar.app/Contents/MacOS/CodexBar`
- macOS: 27.0 (26A5353q)
- View: Overview tab with multiple providers enabled, including Codex and Claude

### Sample

I sampled the app while scrolling the Overview menu:

```sh
sample CodexBar 8 1 -file ~/Desktop/codexbar-overview-scroll-sample.txt
```

The sample points away from provider I/O or background refresh work. The main thread is inside AppKit menu tracking and then spends time in menu layout, SwiftUI rendering, and hit testing while the menu is open.

Relevant stack fragments from the sample:

```text
NSMenuTrackingSession startRunningMenuEventLoop
NSContextMenuImpl _reloadData
NSContextMenuImpl _menuBackingViewDidChangeIntrinsicSizeWithAnimation
NSView layoutSubtreeIfNeeded
ViewGraphRootValueUpdater.render
ViewGraph.renderDisplayList
LazyVGridLayout / HVGrid
Text / CoreText text resolution
NSView scrollWheel
NSContextMenuImpl highlightItemAtEventLocation
NSHostingView.hitTest
```

This makes the issue look like the Overview menu is too heavy for an `NSMenu` tracking surface. Each Overview row is a hosted SwiftUI card, and the detailed usage sections/charts appear to be re-entering layout/render/hit-test work during scroll.

### Why I think this is separate from the older lag issues

I found older reports such as #1196, #1371, and #1387. Those were mostly around earlier menu rebuild/hover/provider-switching regressions and have since been closed. This one is still reproducible on v0.37.0 and is specifically tied to scrolling the Overview tab.

### Possible fix direction

The most likely fix is probably not another refresh debounce. The hot path does not look like provider refresh. A lighter Overview row may be needed: provider name, updated time, plan, and a couple of progress bars in the menu, with the full chart/details kept in the provider tab or submenu. Another option is to keep SwiftUI as the model/source of truth but render the Overview row through a small AppKit bridge with fixed height, cheap hover background, and no full SwiftUI chart invalidation during scroll.

I can share the full `codexbar-overview-scroll-sample.txt` if useful.
target.author: hhh2210
